name: claude-project-planner
description: A structured skill suite for planning IT projects — from initial requirements gathering to architecture, milestones, and tech stack selection.
author: Nikita Manaenkov

skills.name: project-init
skills.description: Stage 1: Initial requirements — guided interview to define project goals, stack, scope, and generate concrete project variants.

skills.name: project-research
skills.description: Stage 2: Competitor research, UX personas, user hypotheses, requirements, value proposition, strategic positioning, interview guide, and user stories. Run after Stage 1.

skills.name: project-interviews
skills.description: Simulated UX interviews — role-plays as each defined persona and answers the interview guide from Stage 2 in first person. Choose how many personas and how deep (brief/standard/deep). Run after Stage 2, before or after Stage 3.

skills.name: project-mvp
skills.description: Stage 3: MVP matrix with Impact/Complexity/Stack Risk evaluation for every user story, release staging from Prototype to final version, primary KPI with formula, advanced economic model, and system positioning. Run after Stage 2.

skills.name: project-sprints
skills.description: Stage 4: Jira board setup (task types, columns, priorities), epic definition, full sprint breakdown with typed tasks (Story/Feature/Spike/Tech Debt/Research/Bug), complete task cards with acceptance criteria and story points, team assignment, and full system architecture with data model, API surface, and ADRs. Run after Stage 3.
